response: The duration for which tests stay active in the HackerRank system is not explicitly mentioned in the provided support documentation. However, you can check the status of a candidate's test by going to the 'Candidates' tab on your home page and searching for the candidate by name or email ID. This will give you the candidate's history, including a Skills summary, the timeline of events (tests and interviews attempted), and their public profile.
justification: The response is based on the 'How can I check a candidate’s attempt using their email?' section of the 'Screen FAQ's' support documentation.

response: Creating test variants allows you to create a single assessment that adapts to different candidate profiles. You can design multiple variants within the same assessment to personalize the evaluation based on roles, skills, or other criteria. This approach eliminates the need to manage separate tests, improves efficiency, and ensures candidates are assessed on the most relevant content. To create test variants, log in to your HackerRank for Work account, go to the Tests tab, open the test you want to modify, and click the more option menu (⋮) in the top right corner. Select Create Variants and then click Create Variants. The system converts the existing test into Variant 1 and opens Variant 2, where you can start creating the second version. To add more variants, select the + icon next to the tabs.
justification: The support ticket asks about best practices for creating variants versus having different tests. The relevant support documentation provides detailed instructions on creating test variants. Ticket contains multiple requests; primary issue addressed, secondary requests may require follow-up.

response: To add extra time for a candidate, log in to your HackerRank for Work account, go to the Tests tab, select the test you want to modify, and then go to the Candidates tab. Select the checkbox next to the candidate(s) you want to accommodate time, click More > Add Time Accommodation, enter the accommodation percentage in multiples of five, and then click Save. The updated duration will appear in the test invite email and on the test landing page before the candidate starts the test.
justification: The support documentation provides step-by-step instructions on how to add extra time for a candidate in a HackerRank assessment. Ticket contains multiple requests; primary issue addressed, secondary requests may require follow-up.
